channel.title: Comments for page "Lending a Hand"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Lending a Hand"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is yet another "meet the team" tale. But it works, and I rather like it. There's action, even if mostly off-screen, which is handled well and serves to move the overall plot forward. There's some minor world-building, clever imagery, and some well-placed documents that serve to set the following scene1.

I wish that Cimmerian had been characterized a bit more strongly than "Ethics Committee Dude Who, We Are Told, Is 'not a naturally emotional person, but he was human,'" though. Still, on the whole, +1

Footnotes
1. Except for "From Duke 'Til Dawn," the Boxbot of Foundation tales

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you look again at 2913's documentation, his containment procedures have been updated. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the use of Foundation training material and so forth between scenes. Also really liked Han in this, especially him wanting to keep the grenade pin. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"How are you going to hide a whole facility?"

Pff. How, he asks. xD

Anyways, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. I actually thought the portrayal was fairly compatible? And one of my big issues with the first draft of this was that it was explicitly different. Could you elaborate, maybe? :D

(The snippets being lighthearted, I thought, went along with the "no one takes the EC seriously except people high-level enough to know better" thing)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. Another great resurrection tale. Nice plot, though there are two minor issues that I have with the tale.
First, the portrayal of the Ethics committee is slightly different from my headcanon, which is basically its portrayal in the Ethics committee orientation tale. Also, the little snippets of supplementary materiel seems too lighthearted for the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Comma inserted.

Beyond that, thank you for the comments. I appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hell he'd been the replacement before."

Comma after "Hell"?

Regardless, a very solid tale and a great addition to Resurrection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to Clef, TheDeadlyMoose, TroyL, Decibelle, Light and all of the other resurrection authors who helped me with or reviewed this piece. It's the longest I've ever worked on a tale and I'm happy with how it turned out.

Edit: Was reminded by a recent interaction that while I'm pretty sure everyone got the Duke Till Dawn reference I stuck in here, I should mention that's where I got the "kiss your ass goodbye" section from for anyone who hadn't read that tale.
